select-columns.label: Selecting Specific Columns
select-columns.summary: How to select only certain columns from a table.

quiz3.title: Write a SQL query to select only the `product_id` and `name` columns from the `products` table.

sketch1.title: Why Select Specific Columns?
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: In SQL, you often don't need all the data from a table. Instead of using `SELECT *`, you can specify which columns you want to retrieve. This makes your queries faster and your results easier to read.

sketch2.title: Syntax for Selecting Columns
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
To select specific columns, list their names separated by commas after the `SELECT` keyword. For example:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products;
```
This query returns only the `name` and `price` columns from the `products` table.

sketch3.title: Benefits of Selecting Columns
sketch3.bodyMarkdown:
- Reduces the amount of data transferred
- Makes results easier to understand
- Improves query performance

Always select only the columns you need.

quiz1.title:
What does the following SQL query do?

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products;
```
quiz1.prompt:
What does the following SQL query do?

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products;
```

options.a: Selects all columns from the products table
options.b: Selects only the name and price columns from the products table
options.c: Deletes the name and price columns from the products table
options.d: Updates the price column in the products table

quiz2.title: Why is it better to select only the columns you need? (Choose all that apply)
quiz2.prompt: Why is it better to select only the columns you need? (Choose all that apply)

options.b: It makes results easier to read
options.d: It reduces the amount of data transferred
